meaning: adj.不能想像的,难以相信的
examples: It sounds inconceivable but is the naked truth.
"It is inconceivable that the euro fails," he said.
Thirty years ago, it would have been inconceivable to have imagined a ban on smoking in the workplace or in pubs, and yet that is what we have now.
In such a state, it is hardly inconceivable that Mr. Sanford is being set-up.
Food scarcity is inconceivable in a country that has had an abundance for so long.
